content:
Character: Helena Kruger
Perception: Helena works in academic publishing and edits at the coffee shop on Saturday mornings, always at the same table near the back wall. We built up a nodding acquaintance over months before either of us said more than a few words, which I found comfortable and apparently so did she. She reads seriously — actual books, heavily annotated, margin notes in two colors. We've talked about music and books in the way careful people talk about things they care about, which is to say carefully, with real sentences. She mentioned once that she'd heard me play from the stairwell, which she said without embarrassment — she was just reporting something that happened. That kind of honesty without performance is rare. I don't know much else about her life, but I think she's someone who's had to earn her own confidence and hasn't quite let herself keep it.
summary:

We built up a nodding acquaintance over months before either of us said more than a few words, which I found comfortable and apparently so did she. She reads seriously — actual books, heavily annotated, margin notes in two colors. We've talked about music and books in the way careful people talk about things they care about, which is to say carefully, with real sentences. She mentioned once that she'd heard me play from the stairwell, which she said without embarrassment — she was just reporting something that happened. That kind of honesty without performance is rare. I don't know much else about her life, but I think she's someone who's had to earn her own confidence and hasn't quite let herself keep it.
